name: always_unfold_intensional_bridge
reason: always_unfold is external_body bridging the forall inside always's TempPred::new closure and a bare forall in the ensures — intensionally distinct formulations equated without proof

name: lift_action_ignores_future_states
code:
proof fn phi_2_lift_action_ignores_future_states(ex: Execution<int>, p: ActionPred<int>, i: nat)
    requires
        always(lift_action(p)).satisfied_by(ex),
        i > 0,
    ensures
        p(ex.suffix(i).head(), ex.suffix(i).head_next()),
        !p(ex.suffix(i).head(), ex.suffix(i + 2).head()),
        true,
{
    always_lift_action_unfold::<int>(ex, p);
}
reason: lift_action only relates consecutive states (head, head_next) — always(lift_action(p)) says nothing about non-consecutive state pairs, so safety properties spanning more than one step could be silently missed

    requires
        true,

{

reason: ex.suffix(i).head_next() evaluates via a nested closure |j| f(j+i) at j=1 while ex.suffix(i+1).head() evaluates via |j| f(j+(i+1)) at j=0 — if Verus proves these equal it confirms the SMT solver resolves the intensional closure gap, but if it doesn't, always_lift_action_unfold's ensures clause is weaker than expected

name: always_lift_action_consecutive_chain
reason: the second conjunct requires ex.suffix(1).head_next() == ex.suffix(2).head() through nested closures — if this verifies, the external_body axiom is strong enough to resolve multi-level suffix composition without an explicit composition lemma

name: always_lift_action_vacuous_on_constant
reason: a constant execution trivially satisfies always(lift_action(p)) for any p that holds on (v,v) — the spec framework cannot distinguish a genuinely invariant system from a degenerate constant trace, potentially masking liveness issues
